title: Golden-config drift after an out-of-window controller push
symptom: config_drift_score stepped up simultaneously across hub1, br1, br2 and br3 with no hardware alarm; path_asymmetry rose as forwarding diverged from intent. The step (not ramp) onset and multi-site simultaneity were the discriminators.
root_cause: An out-of-maintenance-window controller commit pushed a mis-scoped app-route policy to all sites at once, drifting the running configuration away from the golden baseline with no physical fault involved.
contributing_signals: config_drift_score step increase across multiple sites (BOCPD/PELT)
no hardware alarm — discriminator vs A/B/C
resolution: Reverted the offending controller commit to the last-known-good policy and re-pushed golden to the affected sites; config_drift_score returned to zero and metrics normalised. An audit record of the approver was retained and change-management was notified.

timeline.event: BOCPD/PELT step detector + ADWIN fire on config_drift_score across sites; Granger ties it to the controller commit.
Config diff vs golden confirms simultaneous multi-site drift, no hardware alarm.
Offending commit identified from the controller audit log; revert approved.
Last-known-good re-applied; drift score returns to zero.
